meta.maintenanceInstruction: KEEP THIS PLAN UP TO DATE AFTER EVERY SUB-STEP. LOG EVERY DEVIATION, DISCOVERY, AND REPLAN INSIDE THIS FILE BEFORE PROCEEDING. IF REALITY CHANGES, RE-READ THE RELEVANT CODE, THEN REWRITE THE AFFECTED TASKS IMMEDIATELY.
meta.completionPolicy: NO ESTIMATES. NO PRIORITIES. EVERY TASK MUST INCLUDE ITS OWN REPRODUCTION, VALIDATION, STRICT-REVIEW-GATE PASSES, AND A SINGLE SELF-CONTAINED COMMIT BEFORE THE NEXT TASK BEGINS. WHEN THIS LIST IS EXHAUSTED, EXPAND IT AGAIN FROM EVIDENCE, NOT WISHFUL THINKING.

globalAlerts.warning: CRITICAL BUG: fetch.go, mcp.go, mcpcallback.go evaluate adapter.Loop().Promisify UNCONDITIONALLY at module load time. If adapter is nil (valid in tests), this causes immediate segfault/panic. Reference: exec.go uses correct pattern: if adapter != nil { ... adapter.Loop().Promisify ... }

Architectural inconsistency in mcpcallback.go: jsCallbackFactory receives both adapter AND promisify, but derives loop from adapter internally. The promisify parameter is redundant since adapter.Loop().Promisify is available from adapter.
DRY violation in mcp.go: mcpServer struct stores adapter, loop, AND promisify — all three are redundant since adapter.Loop() and adapter.Loop().Promisify can be derived at call time.

mandatoryDirectives.contextManagement: Read blueprint.json and WIP.md on every session start. Resume from the first non-Done task.
mandatoryDirectives.toolingConstraints: Use gmake on macOS. Primary signals: make test-short and make lint.

statusSection.currentState: All review.md findings addressed: fetch.go, mcp.go, mcpcallback.go all use if adapter != nil guards before adapter.Loop(). mcpcallback.jsCallbackFactory refactored to derive promisify internally. mcp.go mcpServer struct simplified to store only adapter. Rule of Two: TWO CONTIGUOUS ISSUE-FREE PASSES. Tests pass. Lint clean. Build succeeds.
statusSection.goalState: All native modules handle nil adapter gracefully. fetch.go, mcp.go, mcpcallback.go use the same guard pattern as exec.go. mcpcallback.go refactored to derive promisify from adapter internally (remove redundant parameter). mcp.go struct simplified to store only adapter (derive loop and promisify at call time). Two contiguous issue-free subagent reviews confirm all fixes.

replanLog.trigger: User directive to address scratch/review.md findings via /blueprint-json
replanLog.affectedTasks: All tasks superseded — new plan focused on review.md critical defects
replanLog.summary: replan triggered by review.md: CRITICAL nil-pointer dereference bugs in fetch.go, mcp.go, mcpcallback.go. Need to apply the if adapter != nil guard pattern from exec.go. Secondary: architectural redundancy fixes in mcpcallback.go and mcp.go.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix fetch.go nil adapter panic: add guard before adapter.Loop().Promisify
sequentialTasks.acceptance: go test -short ./internal/builtin/fetch/... passes. The test that passes nil adapter (analogous to pr_split_test.go pattern) no longer panics at module initialization — it only panics if fetch/sseReader are actually called, which is acceptable.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix mcp.go nil adapter panic: add guard before adapter.Loop().Promisify
sequentialTasks.acceptance: go test -short ./internal/builtin/mcpmod/... passes. Module loads safely with nil adapter; crashes only if createServer is called, matching exec.go behavior.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix mcpcallback.go nil adapter panic: add guard before adapter.Loop().Promisify
sequentialTasks.acceptance: go test -short ./internal/builtin/mcpcallbackmod/... passes. Module loads safely with nil adapter.

sequentialTasks.task: Refactor mcpcallback.go: remove redundant promisify parameter from jsCallbackFactory
sequentialTasks.description:
In [internal/builtin/mcpcallbackmod/mcpcallback.go], jsCallbackFactory receives both adapter and promisify as parameters, but derives loop from adapter internally (line 154: loop := adapter.Loop()). Since adapter is already passed and provides adapter.Loop().Promisify, the promisify parameter is redundant.

Remove the promisify parameter entirely. Update jsCallbackFactory signature from:
  func jsCallbackFactory(rt *goja.Runtime, adapter *gojaeventloop.Adapter, promisify PromisifyFunc) func(call goja.FunctionCall) goja.Value
To:
  func jsCallbackFactory(rt *goja.Runtime, adapter *gojaeventloop.Adapter) func(call goja.FunctionCall) goja.Value

Update the mcpCallback struct to derive promisify from adapter at construction time (line 204), and update all callers (jsInit, jsInitSync, jsClose, acceptLoop) to use cb.loop.Promisify or store promisify from cb.promisify field as before. The field stays, but is set from adapter.Loop().Promisify in the constructor rather than passed as a parameter.

Also fix the Require call to remove the redundant argument:
  _ = exports.Set("MCPCallback", jsCallbackFactory(rt, adapter))
sequentialTasks.acceptance: go test -short ./internal/builtin/mcpcallbackmod/... passes. jsCallbackFactory no longer receives promisify as a separate parameter. The struct's promisify field is set from adapter.Loop().Promisify in the constructor. No behavioral change.

sequentialTasks.task: Refactor mcp.go mcpServer struct: remove redundant loop and promisify fields
sequentialTasks.description:
In [internal/builtin/mcpmod/mcp.go], the mcpServer struct stores all three: adapter, loop, and promisify. Since adapter.Loop() and adapter.Loop().Promisify can be derived at call time, loop and promisify are redundant storage.

Simplify the struct to store only adapter:
  type mcpServer struct {
      server  *mcp.Server
      adapter *gojaeventloop.Adapter  // ONLY this one — loop and promisify derived below
      runtime *goja.Runtime
      mu      sync.Mutex
      running bool
      cancel  context.CancelFunc
  }

Remove the loop and promisify fields entirely. Update jsCreateServer to NOT store them:
  s := &mcpServer{
      server:  srv,
      adapter: adapter,
      runtime: runtime,
  }

Update all internal usages:
  - makeToolHandler: s.loop.Submit(...) → s.adapter.Loop().Submit(...)
  - jsRun: s.promisify(...) → s.adapter.Loop().Promisify(...); s.loop.Submit(...) → s.adapter.Loop().Submit(...)
sequentialTasks.acceptance: go test -short ./internal/builtin/mcpmod/... passes. mcpServer struct stores only adapter, loop and promisify are derived at call time. No behavioral change.

sequentialTasks.task: Rule of Two: First subagent review of all fixes
sequentialTasks.description:
Invoke a subagent to perform the first review pass on all changes made in Tasks 1-5. The subagent must verify:
1. fetch.go, mcp.go, mcpcallback.go all use if adapter != nil guards before adapter.Loop()
2. mcpcallback.go jsCallbackFactory signature changed (promisify parameter removed)
3. mcp.go mcpServer struct simplified (adapter only, loop/promisify removed)
4. All existing tests pass: go test -short -race ./internal/builtin/fetch/... ./internal/builtin/mcpmod/... ./internal/builtin/mcpcallbackmod/...
5. No regressions introduced by the refactors

The subagent should be skeptical and hostile to errors. Report ALL findings.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: First subagent review completes with zero blocking issues. If any issues are found, they are logged and the process resets to the relevant task.

sequentialTasks.task: Rule of Two: Second subagent review of all fixes
sequentialTasks.description:
Invoke a NEW subagent (fresh, no memory of Pass 1) to perform the second review pass on the identical diff. Verify the same checklist as Pass 1. This pass must be independent and equally skeptical.

The subagent must verify:
1. All nil-guard patterns are correctly implemented in fetch.go, mcp.go, mcpcallback.go
2. mcpcallback.jsCallbackFactory refactor is complete and consistent
3. mcp.go mcpServer struct refactor is complete and consistent
4. All tests pass: go test -short -race ./internal/builtin/fetch/... ./internal/builtin/mcpmod/... ./internal/builtin/mcpcallbackmod/...
5. No regressions
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Second subagent review completes with zero blocking issues. This confirms the code is at the local maximum of quality.

sequentialTasks.description:
Run the full verification suite:
- go test -short -race ./internal/builtin/fetch/... ./internal/builtin/mcpmod/... ./internal/builtin/mcpcallbackmod/...
- make lint
- make build

Then create a self-contained commit with the fix. The commit message must reference scratch/review.md and the specific defects addressed.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Full test suite passes, lint clean, build succeeds. Single commit created referencing the review.md findings. Blueprint updated to reflect completion.

continuousVerification.task: Run make test-short and make lint after every significant change
